contract.goal: Build the LinearClient + auth resolution + structured-error foundation that subsequent Phase 3 verbs share, and ship the configure verb (DESIGN.md § 10) as the first wired verb. Configure validates auth + Linear Project ID existence; labels/custom-fields/Document-templates from § 10 are deferred (underspec'd vs Linear's data model) and surfaced explicitly in the response shape.
acceptance_criteria: cli/lib/auth.ts: resolveAuth({env?, fileReader?, homeDirResolver?}) resolves env first, file fallback. Structured errors per failure mode.
cli/lib/linear-client.ts: LinearClient.query<T>() POSTs to api.linear.app/graphql with Authorization: <apiKey> (no Bearer). Retries 429/5xx/network. 401/403 → immediate auth-refused.
cli/verbs/configure.ts: async verb; --linear-project=<id> required; queries Linear; returns structured success/error. bootstrapped field names what's done vs deferred.
cli/linear-loom.ts: wires configure into VERBLESS_HANDLERS. Async main(); unhandled-error envelope.
Unit tests for each module pass under npm test.
CLI smoke: configure without --linear-project errors with structured missing-linear-project JSON exit 1; --help still works.
rules_applied: DESIGN.md § 9 (auth: LINEAR_API_KEY env primary, ~/.linear-loom/config.json fallback — auth only, no defaults).
DESIGN.md § 10 (idempotent schema bootstrap — trivially idempotent in v1 because labels/fields/templates are deferred).
DESIGN.md § 19 (output-stability contract; configure's success shape is the start of the linear-loom output contract).
Loom precedent: LoomError shape mirrored. Dependency-injection pattern mirroring ctx.ghRunner.
disqualifiers: Adding npm runtime dependencies (built-in fetch only).
Adding tsconfig.json (no other plugin has one).
Implementing labels/custom-fields/Document-template work (explicitly deferred and surfaced).
inputs: Linear GraphQL API endpoint convention (Authorization: <apiKey>, no Bearer prefix)

actions: Surveyed loom's cli/lib/ layout (errors.ts, gh.ts) and verbs/loom/ structure (DispatchResult, errToResult patterns) to keep linear-loom's shape parallel.
Wrote cli/lib/errors.ts: LinearLoomError class extending Error; carries code, candidates, namespace, verb; toPayload() emits the structured stderr envelope.

Wrote cli/lib/auth.ts: resolveAuth() with dependency-injected env/fileReader/homeDirResolver; env-first LINEAR_API_KEY path; ~/.linear-loom/config.json fallback validated for shape ({api_key: non-empty string}); each failure mode throws a distinct LinearLoomError code.

Wrote cli/lib/linear-client.ts: LinearClient class with constructor-validated apiKey, DI'd fetchFn + sleepFn, POSTs GraphQL to Linear's endpoint with raw Authorization header; retries 429/5xx/network errors with exponential backoff (defaults 4 attempts, 500ms base); 401/403 short-circuit to auth-refused; graphql-error vs graphql-empty distinguishes response shapes.

Wrote cli/verbs/configure.ts: async verb dispatching against --linear-project=<id>; resolves auth via injectable resolveAuthFn; queries Linear for { project(id) { id name } }; returns structured success payload with bootstrapped field naming each deferral with rationale strings (labels: deferred-no-system-managed-labels-at-configure-scope; custom_fields: none-in-v1; document_templates: deferred-to-phase-4-mirroring).
Modified cli/linear-loom.ts: introduced VERBLESS_HANDLERS map; main() is now async; configure dispatches through; uncaught errors emit unhandled-error JSON with exit 3.

Wrote 4 colocated *.test.ts files covering: 4 LinearLoomError tests, 9 resolveAuth tests (happy path + every documented error code), 9 LinearClient tests (happy, 429 retry with backoff verification, 5xx exhaust, 401 immediate fail, graphql-error, graphql-empty, network-error), 6 configure tests (missing/empty flag, happy path, project-not-found, auth-error pass-through, --pretty). 28 new tests total.
Verified: npm test → 673 passing (was 645; +28). plugins/linear-loom/bin/linear-loom configure exits 1 with structured missing-linear-project JSON. --help still works.
corrections: Mid-development: the 'surfaces resolveAuth errors as structured stderr' test used a fake error-object that wasn't a LinearLoomError instance, so configure's errToResult instanceof check rejected it and the error propagated. Fixed by using new LinearLoomError(...) directly in the test.

changes_since_previous: First unit on the ev-agent.linear-loom.project-verbs branch (PR #34 for Phase 2 merged earlier this session). This commit also picks up U2-of-Phase-2's loom pr update orphan event in events.jsonl — that event lagged the Phase 2 unit-commit boundary per the known substrate gap.
notes_for_pr: First Phase 3 unit. Builds the substrate-shared LinearClient + auth + errors foundation that U2/U3/U4 will consume. The dependency-injection pattern (LinearClient takes fetchFn + sleepFn; verb handlers take ctx with optional client + resolveAuthFn) keeps tests mock-friendly and matches loom's ghRunner pattern.

configure v1 scope is auth-and-existence-check only. The 'bootstrapped' payload field names each piece of § 10's nominal scope (labels, custom fields, Document templates) with an explicit deferral rationale string — this is the substrate's first 'in-band deferral marker' shape. Phase 4 (mirroring verbs) might harden these from human-readable strings into a typed {status: 'deferred', reason: string} enum; v1 keeps them as strings to surface intent without committing to a schema until there's a real consumer (observation surfaced by contract-fit evaluator).

Linear's API was reached via built-in fetch + raw GraphQL strings — no SDK dependency. Matches the family's zero-runtime-deps posture. The minimal client surface (`query<T>` method, exponential backoff on transient failures) is intentional — the broader Linear SDK surface (Issue, Project, Label-specific helpers) would be premature.

Substrate-followup candidate: derive-panel keeps returning evaluator-react-api on the .ts files in this artifact (none of which import React). Per PANEL-COMPOSITION.md row 42, react-api applies to .ts only when the file imports React — so the script doesn't check imports and over-applies. The L-004-style manual override path (the skill body's auto-derivation step's import-checking) is the right substrate fix.

events.jsonl in this unit's commit carries the orphan pr-updated event from U3 of Phase 2's loom pr update call (the same pr-opened/pr-updated lag pattern documented in the substrate memory). Forward-only carry: no retroactive bookkeeping.
